title: Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens
summary: Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens retrieves prior skills in an original independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css custom property definition, css custom property cascade, css custom property fallbacks, css design token system, css property registration in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Developer Field Manual token-system work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the Developer Field Manual token-system work here. */

steps.title: 01 · debug 1: field manual spacing and color value trace · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions.

content.title: independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions evidence
content.text: For Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens, choose the css custom property definition claim that preserves the independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 1: custom properties are replaced before the cascade and never inherit.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 1: trace the winning custom-property declaration through cascade and inheritance, then validate the resolved token stream in the consuming property.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 1: treat --manual-gap and --Manual-gap as the same variable because CSS property names ignore case.

Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 2: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 2: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 2: a custom property declared on root cannot be overridden safely inside a component.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 2: scope defaults broadly, override semantic inputs on the smallest intended subtree, and inspect the value at the consumer element.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 2: use important on root so local components cannot accidentally customize their own inputs.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 3: a var fallback repairs every syntactically invalid resolved value.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 3: use nested fallbacks for genuinely missing tokens, then separately test present-but-invalid values in the consuming property.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 3: add more nested fallbacks until any invalid declaration becomes valid automatically.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 4: every raw CSS value should become a token regardless of reuse or meaning.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 4: tokenize durable decisions, map primitives to semantic roles, expose narrow component inputs, and verify every theme and preference state.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 4: create a separate copy of each component for dark mode to avoid cascade interactions.
